original: NaxConfig.acceptance.fix has type AcceptanceFixConfig with fields diagnoseModel (string), fixModel (string), strategy ('diagnose-first' | 'implement-only'), maxRetries (number)
refined: TypeScript type check passes: NaxConfig.acceptance has property 'fix' of type AcceptanceFixConfig with fields diagnoseModel (string), fixModel (string), strategy ('diagnose-first'|'implement-only'), maxRetries (number)

original: DEFAULT_CONFIG.acceptance.fix equals { diagnoseModel: 'fast', fixModel: 'balanced', strategy: 'diagnose-first', maxRetries: 2 }
refined: DEFAULT_CONFIG.acceptance.fix is deep-equal to { diagnoseModel: 'fast', fixModel: 'balanced', strategy: 'diagnose-first', maxRetries: 2 }

original: DiagnosisResult interface exported from src/acceptance/types.ts with required fields verdict, reasoning, confidence and optional fields testIssues, sourceIssues
refined: TypeScript module src/acceptance/types.ts exports DiagnosisResult interface with required fields verdict: 'source_bug'|'test_bug'|'both', reasoning: string, confidence: number, and optional fields testIssues?: string[], sourceIssues?: string[]

original: Config validation rejects strategy values other than 'diagnose-first' and 'implement-only'
refined: NaxConfigSchema.parse({ acceptance: { fix: { strategy: 'invalid' } } }) throws Zod validation error; NaxConfigSchema.parse({ acceptance: { fix: { strategy: 'diagnose-first' } } }) succeeds; NaxConfigSchema.parse({ acceptance: { fix: { strategy: 'implement-only' } } }) succeeds

original: diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() receives the agent adapter via parameter (from ctx.agentGetFn in caller) — never calls bare getAgent()
refined: Function signature includes agentAdapter parameter; function body does not contain getAgent() call; agentAdapter.run() is invoked instead

original: diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() calls agent.run() (not agent.complete()) with sessionRole 'diagnose'
refined: agentAdapter.run() is called with sessionRole: 'diagnose' in arguments object; agentAdapter.complete() is never called

original: Session name follows pattern nax-<hash>-<feature>-<storyId>-diagnose via buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, 'diagnose')
refined: sessionName matches regex /^nax-[a-f0-9]+-.+-\d+-diagnose$/; buildSessionName is called with suffix 'diagnose'

original: diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() resolves diagnoseModel tier from config.acceptance.fix.diagnoseModel via resolveModelForAgent() — never passes raw tier name
refined: resolveModelForAgent(config.acceptance.fix.diagnoseModel) is called; raw tier string is not passed to agentAdapter.run(); resolved model is passed instead

original: diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() auto-detects source file paths from import statements in the test file content and includes their content in the prompt (up to 5 files, 500 lines each)
refined: prompt includes content from ≤5 files; each file has ≤500 lines; extracted from import/export statements in test file; source files are read via fs.readFileSync

original: When agent output contains valid JSON matching DiagnosisResult schema, diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() returns the parsed result
refined: Returns object with keys: verdict (string), reasoning (string), confidence (number), fixPlan (string|undefined); JSON.parse succeeds on agent output; schema validation passes

original: When agent output is invalid or unparsable, diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() returns { verdict: 'source_bug', reasoning: 'diagnosis failed — falling back to source fix', confidence: 0 }
refined: On JSON.parse failure or schema validation failure, returns exact object: {verdict: 'source_bug', reasoning: 'diagnosis failed — falling back to source fix', confidence: 0}

original: When adapter.run() throws an error, diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() catches it and returns the fallback DiagnosisResult
refined: try/catch wraps agentAdapter.run() call; on thrown error, returns fallback DiagnosisResult with verdict: 'source_bug', confidence: 0, reasoning contains error message

original: When config.agent.protocol is 'acp', the diagnosis session runs as a proper ACP session visible in acpx list
refined: When protocol === 'acp', session appears in acpx sessions list with name matching nax-<hash>-<feature>-<storyId>-diagnose pattern; acpx session-ls returns this session within 5s of diagnosis start

original: executeSourceFix() receives the agent adapter via parameter (from ctx.agentGetFn in caller) — never calls bare getAgent()
refined: Function executeSourceFix has a parameter named 'agent' of type AgentAdapter; the function body contains no calls to getAgent() or agentGetFn()

original: executeSourceFix() calls agent.run() (not agent.complete(), not pipeline) with sessionRole 'source-fix'
refined: agent.run() is called within executeSourceFix with sessionRole string equal to 'source-fix'; agent.complete() and pipeline calls are absent from executeSourceFix body

original: Session name follows pattern nax-<hash>-<feature>-<storyId>-source-fix via buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, 'source-fix')
refined: buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, 'source-fix') is called and its return value is passed as sessionName to agent.run(); returned string matches /^nax-[a-f0-9]+-.+-.*-source-fix$/

original: executeSourceFix() resolves fixModel tier from config.acceptance.fix.fixModel via resolveModelForAgent() — never passes raw tier name
refined: resolveModelForAgent(config.acceptance.fix.fixModel) is called and its result is passed to agent.run(); no raw tier string is passed directly to agent.run()

original: executeSourceFix() includes failing test output and diagnosis reasoning in the agent prompt
refined: The prompt string passed to agent.run() contains substring 'failing test' and contains substring 'diagnosis' or 'reasoning'; prompt is a single concatenated string argument to agent.run()

original: executeSourceFix() does NOT use the pipeline — calls adapter.run() directly, then verifies by running bun test <acceptance-test-path>
refined: executeSourceFix does not reference Pipeline or pipeline.execute; Bun.spawn or Bun.secondary's args array includes 'bun test <acceptance-test-path>' after adapter.run() completes

original: executeSourceFix() returns { success: boolean, cost: number } where cost is extracted from AgentResult
refined: Return type annotation is { success: boolean; cost: number }; cost value is sourced from result.cost or result.usage?.cost on the AgentResult returned by agent.run()

original: When config.agent.protocol is 'acp', the fix session runs as a proper ACP session visible in acpx list with correct session name
refined: When config.agent.protocol === 'acp', the session name passed to agent.run() appears in output of `acpx list` command after session creation

original: acceptance-loop.ts obtains agent via (ctx.agentGetFn ?? _acceptanceLoopDeps.getAgent)(agentName) and passes it to diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() and executeSourceFix() — never bare getAgent()
refined: acceptance-loop.ts obtains agent via (ctx.agentGetFn ?? _acceptanceLoopDeps.getAgent)(agentName) and passes it to diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() and executeSourceFix() — never bare getAgent()

original: When strategy is 'diagnose-first' and diagnosis verdict is 'source_bug', acceptance-loop calls executeSourceFix() (which uses agent.run() with sessionRole 'source-fix') instead of generateAndAddFixStories()
refined: When strategy is 'diagnose-first' and diagnosis verdict is 'source_bug', acceptance-loop calls executeSourceFix() (which uses agent.run() with sessionRole 'source-fix') instead of generateAndAddFixStories()

original: When strategy is 'diagnose-first' and diagnosis verdict is 'test_bug', acceptance-loop calls regenerateAcceptanceTest() then re-runs acceptance validation
refined: When strategy is 'diagnose-first' and diagnosis verdict is 'test_bug', acceptance-loop calls regenerateAcceptanceTest() then re-runs acceptance validation

original: When strategy is 'diagnose-first' and diagnosis verdict is 'both', acceptance-loop calls executeSourceFix() first, then calls regenerateAcceptanceTest() if acceptance still fails
refined: When strategy is 'diagnose-first' and diagnosis verdict is 'both', acceptance-loop calls executeSourceFix() first, then calls regenerateAcceptanceTest() if acceptance still fails

original: When strategy is 'implement-only', acceptance-loop skips diagnosis and calls executeSourceFix() directly
refined: When strategy is 'implement-only', acceptance-loop skips diagnosis and calls executeSourceFix() directly

original: Fix retries respect config.acceptance.fix.maxRetries (separate from config.acceptance.maxRetries)
refined: Fix retries respect config.acceptance.fix.maxRetries (separate from config.acceptance.maxRetries)

original: JSONL event with stage 'acceptance.diagnosis' emitted containing verdict and confidence fields
refined: JSONL event with stage 'acceptance.diagnosis' emitted containing verdict and confidence fields

original: JSONL event with stage 'acceptance.source-fix' emitted containing cost and success fields
refined: JSONL event with stage 'acceptance.source-fix' emitted containing cost and success fields

original: JSONL event with stage 'acceptance.test-regen' emitted containing outcome field
refined: JSONL event with stage 'acceptance.test-regen' emitted containing outcome field
